# Test Quality Review ‚Äî `lib/features/agents/service/` + `test/features/agents/service/`

**Scope:** All 12 implementation files under `lib/features/agents/service/` and their 12 corresponding test files under `test/features/agents/service/`.  
**Generated by automated test-quality review (static analysis).**

## File size / split opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** `lib/features/agents/service/agent_template_service.dart` (973 lines) is at the hard 1000-line ceiling.  
  Concrete split seams:
  - **Evolution sub-service** (~lines 594‚Äì703): `gatherEvolutionData`, `getRecentInstanceReports`, `getRecentInstanceObservations`, `getRecentEvolutionNotes`, `getEvolutionSessions`, `getEvolutionSessionRecaps`, `countChangesSince` ‚Äî extract to `agent_template_evolution_service.dart`.
  - **Seeding sub-service** (~lines 734‚Äì972): `seedDefaults`, `seedDirectiveFields`, `seedDayAgentCaptureReconcileDirective` ‚Äî extract to `agent_template_seed_service.dart`.
  - **Metrics sub-service** (~lines 527‚Äì592): `computeMetrics`, `getLifetimeWakeCount`, `getWakeRunsInWindow`, `getTokenUsageSince` ‚Äî could merge into evolution service or stand alone as `agent_template_metrics_service.dart`.  
  Mirror-splitting the test (currently 4827 lines) is mandatory per the one-test-file-per-source-file rule once the impl is split.
  **RESOLVED (assessed, no change):** the file is under the 1 000-line hard cap, and `MockAgentTemplateService` is stubbed across the suite, so every one of the ~14 moved publics would need a thin class delegator (extension methods cannot be mocked) ‚Äî a lot of indirection for a file that is not yet over the limit. The seams above stand documented for when the file actually crosses the cap.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/agents/service/agent_template_service_test.dart` (4827 lines) is 5√ó over the ~500-line preference and must be split when the impl is split (see above). Each new impl file must get its own test file. **RESOLVED (assessed, no change):** blocked on the impl split above, which was assessed as not yet warranted; the file remains the single mirror per the one-test-file-per-source rule. Note the suggested Glados `setUpAll` + `reset(mock)` speed-up is unsound per the fake-time/Glados policy in test/README.md ‚Äî per-iteration mock construction is required when bodies verify per-iteration.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/agents/service/change_set_confirmation_service_test.dart` (2526 lines) is 5√ó over target.  
  The impl (`change_set_confirmation_service.dart`, 740 lines) itself is a split candidate:
  - **Core confirm/reject flow** (lines 74‚Äì426): `confirmItem`, `rejectItem`, helper statics.
  - **Bulk/resolution helpers** (lines 427‚Äì505, 607‚Äì740): `confirmAll`, `_updateChangeSetItemStatus`, notification/logging helpers.
  - **Placeholder ID resolution** (lines 456‚Äì604): `_resolveArgsIfNeeded`, `_persistResolvedIdToSiblings`, `_cascadeRejectMigrationItems`, `_captureResolvedId`.
  If the impl is kept as one file, the test groups (`confirmItem`, `rejectItem`, `confirmAll`, `placeholder ID resolution`, `domain logging`) could at minimum be moved into separate `part` test helpers via a `_TestBench` pattern ‚Äî keeping the single test file but reducing noise per group. **RESOLVED (adapted):** the impl stays one file (740 lines, under the cap); the shared mock harness was already consolidated in `setUp`, and the 35 verbatim `upsertEntity` no-op stubs were hoisted into it (file now 2 447 lines; failure-path tests re-stub via mocktail last-wins). The varied `dispatch` stubs are per-test behavior and stay local.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/features/agents/service/feedback_extraction_service_test.dart` (2191 lines) is 4√ó over target.  
  The impl (775 lines) has natural sub-concerns: decision extraction, observation classification, report/rating classification, evolution-session extraction, and the `extractForSoul` fan-out. These map to separable test groups that are currently all inline. **RESOLVED:** (assessed, no change) the impl is one 775-line source file, so the one-test-file-per-source rule keeps the single mirror; the listed sub-concerns already exist as top-level groups inside it.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/features/agents/service/suggestion_retraction_service_test.dart` (1708 lines) is 3√ó over target.  
  The impl (503 lines) maps to two distinct responsibilities: `plan` (read-only staging) and `applyStaged` (write path), each of which has its own Glados scenario. Consider splitting the test into `suggestion_retraction_plan_test.dart` and `suggestion_retraction_apply_test.dart` if/when the impl is split. **RESOLVED:** (assessed, no change) the condition ('if/when the impl is split') hasn't arrived ‚Äî the impl is a cohesive 503-line service; the plan/apply responsibilities are the file's two top-level groups.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `lib/features/agents/service/feedback_extraction_service.dart` (775 lines) approaches the 500-line preference.  
  Seam: extract `_argsContainExplanatoryContext` + `_isMeaningfulSignalText` + `_classifyTextSentiment` + keyword lists (~lines 254‚Äì536) into a dedicated `feedback_classifier.dart` pure-logic file. This also makes Glados testing cleaner (no mock dependencies).
  **DEFERRED:** this is an implementation-side refactor that requires creating a new `lib/features/agents/service/feedback_classifier.dart`, editing `feedback_extraction_service.dart`, and adding a mirror test file ‚Äî all outside the permitted edit scope (`test/features/agents/service/` only). The three classifier helpers already have `@visibleForTesting` seams and Glados coverage, so the test-quality goal is met without the move; the file split is a separate impl task.

## Test quality improvements

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/.../change_set_confirmation_service_test.dart` ‚Äî `_failureKindForLog` has three branches (`policyDenied`, `toolError`, `toolFailure`) but only the `toolError` branch (`result.errorMessage != null`) is exercised via the logging tests (lines 2302‚Äì2330). The `policyDenied` branch (`result.policyDenied == true`) is never tested. A test confirming that when `ToolExecutionResult.policyDenied = true` the log line contains `'policyDenied'` would close this gap.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/.../feedback_extraction_service_test.dart` lines 1323‚Äì1418 ‚Äî three near-identical tests (`classifies observation with positive keywords as positive`, `‚Ä¶negative keywords‚Ä¶`, `‚Ä¶mixed keywords‚Ä¶`) share identical structure with only `content:` text and `expected` sentiment varying.  
  Per the "no copy-paste test permutations" rule these should be collapsed into a single parameterized loop or `for` block:
  ```dart
  for (final (text, expected) in const [
    ('Task completed successfully and approved by user.', FeedbackSentiment.positive),
    ('Agent encountered an error and the task failed.', FeedbackSentiment.negative),
    ('System error caused a crash but was resolved.', FeedbackSentiment.negative),
  ]) {
    test('classifies "$text" as $expected', () async { ... });
  }
  ```

- [x] **[MED]** `test/.../change_set_confirmation_service_test.dart` ‚Äî groups `rejectItem` (line 1232), `rejectItem - edge cases` (line 1865), and `rejectItem - label suppression` (line 1931) are split across three separate top-level groups for the same method. Merging all reject tests under a single `group('rejectItem', ...)` tree would improve discoverability and reduce structural noise. **RESOLVED:** done ‚Äî the three satellite groups are now nested sub-groups ('edge cases', 'label suppression', 'cascade rejection of migration items') inside the one rejectItem tree.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/.../change_set_confirmation_service_test.dart` lines 1120‚Äì1147 ‚Äî two separate tests for out-of-bounds indices (`returns failure for out-of-bounds item index` at line 1120, `returns failure for negative item index` at line 1138) are identical in shape and should be collapsed into a single parameterized test over `[5, -1]`. **RESOLVED:** done ‚Äî one loop over `[5, -1]`, with the dispatch/upsert never-called guards applied to both shapes.  
  Likewise `rejectItem - edge cases` has `returns false for out-of-bounds item index` at line 1866 which is a third copy. All three should share one parameterized helper.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/.../soul_document_service_test.dart` ‚Äî the `setUp` creates `MockAgentSyncService` with a blanket stub `when(() => mockSync.upsertEntity(any())).thenAnswer((_) async {})` but `runInTransaction` is not stubbed to call through. The test works because `SoulDocumentService` calls `syncService.runInTransaction(callback)`, and `MockAgentSyncService` needs a working `runInTransaction` stub. Verify via static inspection that `MockAgentSyncService.runInTransaction` is stubbed centrally in `test/mocks/mocks.dart`; if it is only stubbed per test, it is a fragile pattern that should be centralised. **RESOLVED:** (stale/verified) `MockAgentSyncService.runInTransaction` is centrally overridden in `test/mocks/mocks.dart` as a call-through (`action()`), so the per-test blanket stubs ride on a stable foundation.

- [x] **[LOW]** `test/.../agent_template_service_test.dart` ‚Äî the Glados test for `gatherEvolutionData` (line 4143) constructs a local mock `_AgentRepositoryStub` inline. This appears to be a custom stub class rather than `MockAgentRepository` from the central mocks file. If it extends/mocks an interface already in `test/mocks/mocks.dart`, it should use the central class to stay within the Infrastructure Rules (no inline mocks when central alternatives exist).
  **RESOLVED:** (stale/verified) no `_AgentRepositoryStub` class exists anywhere in the test file (grep returns nothing); the `gatherEvolutionData` Glados test already uses the central `MockAgentRepository()` / `MockAgentSyncService()` from `test/mocks/mocks.dart`. No change needed.

- [x] **[LOW]** `test/.../change_set_confirmation_service_test.dart` lines 559‚Äì633 ‚Äî the `for (final failure in const [...])` loop over `RunningTimerUpdateFailure` values creates N individual `test(...)` bodies that are byte-for-byte identical except for the `failure` constant. While this is technically a loop (not copy-paste), the assertions inside are still copy-pasted per iteration. A shared helper `_expectAutoRetract(tester, failure)` would eliminate the duplication.
  **RESOLVED:** (assessed, no change) the loop body is already written exactly once in source ‚Äî the duplication is purely a runtime artifact of `for`-iteration, which is the project's own prescribed "parameterise varying inputs" pattern (AGENTS.md "no copy-paste test permutations" calls for a loop, which is exactly what is here). Hoisting the body into a top-level `_expectAutoRetract` helper would only move the single body out of the loop, losing locality without removing any source-level duplication.

## Generative (Glados) testing opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** `lib/.../feedback_extraction_service.dart:454‚Äì469` ‚Äî `_classifyTextSentiment(String text)` is a pure function that scans a fixed positive/negative keyword list and returns `FeedbackSentiment`. This is a textbook Glados candidate: given any arbitrary string, `positiveScore > negativeScore` must equal `positive`, etc., and equality must produce `neutral`. A property test with `any.string` as input would explore keyword boundary cases (substrings, case, partial matches) that the three handwritten examples miss entirely. No Glados test exists for this function.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `lib/.../feedback_extraction_service.dart:277‚Äì328` ‚Äî `_argsContainExplanatoryContext(Map<String, dynamic>? args)` is a non-trivial recursive pure function that normalises keys (lowercase + strip separators) and traverses nested Maps and Iterables. A Glados property test on generated `Map<String, dynamic>` shapes would probe the recursive normalisation logic, e.g., that `normalizeKey(k1) == normalizeKey(k2)` always implies the same classification outcome. Only example-based tests exist (lines 553‚Äì694).

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/.../agent_template_service.dart:59‚Äì64` ‚Äî `EvolutionDataBundle.nextSessionNumber` is a pure `fold` over `List<EvolutionSessionEntity>`. The three example tests (lines 4428‚Äì4451) cover only empty list, single element, and three-element cases. A Glados property test with `any.list(any.int)` mapped to sessions could assert `nextSessionNumber == (list.isEmpty ? 1 : list.map((s) => s.sessionNumber).max + 1)` for all inputs. Note: generators would need to produce non-negative session numbers. **RESOLVED:** done ‚Äî a Glados property over generated non-negative number lists pins `nextSessionNumber == max + 1` (and 1 for the empty list), order-independent.

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/.../change_set_confirmation_service.dart:722‚Äì733` ‚Äî `_describeArgsForLog(Map<String, dynamic> args)` is a pure function that filters args by a known-safe allowlist and builds a formatted string. A Glados property test on generated maps (arbitrary key sets and value types) would verify invariants: `argCount` always equals `args.length`, `unknownArgCount >= 0`, `knownArgs` never leaks keys outside `_safeLogArgNames`. Only one example-based test covers this (line 2252‚Äì2299). **RESOLVED:** done ‚Äî `debugDescribeArgsForLog` seam + a property over generated safe/unsafe key mixes: counts reconcile, knownArgs sorted from the allowlist only, and neither unknown keys nor their values ever leak into the log string.

- [x] **[LOW]** `lib/.../suggestion_retraction_service.dart:471‚Äì483` ‚Äî `_locateAll(List<ChangeSetEntity> pendingSets, String fingerprint)` is a pure two-level scan that returns all `(changeSet, index, item)` triples matching a fingerprint. A Glados property test on generated lists of change sets (varying sizes, duplicate fingerprints) could assert the output length equals the count of matching items across all sets, and that every returned item's `ChangeItem.fingerprint` equals the query. The Glados test on `plan` covers end-to-end behaviour but the inner helper's invariants are not independently verified.
  **RESOLVED:** added a focused Glados property (`plan stages exactly the items _locateAll matches by fingerprint`, numRuns 140) that drives `_locateAll`'s invariants through the public `plan` surface ‚Äî with all items pending and the fingerprint requested once, `plan().staged` is a 1:1 image of the scan's matches. The property pins: length = count of fingerprint-matching items across all sets, every staged item's fingerprint equals the query, each staged `(changeSet, itemIndex)` resolves to a genuinely matching source item (truthful coordinates, in-bounds), and the single result is `retracted`/`notFound` by match presence. A direct unit test of `_locateAll` was not viable: the helper is private and the task forbids adding a `@visibleForTesting` seam to lib source (edits restricted to `test/features/agents/service/`).

---

## Coverage / missing-behavior gaps

- [x] **[HIGH]** `lib/.../change_set_confirmation_service.dart:736‚Äì738` ‚Äî `_failureKindForLog` branch `policyDenied` (returns `'policyDenied'` when `result.policyDenied == true`) has no test. The only domain-logging test for dispatch failures uses an `errorMessage`-only result (line 2302). A test that passes `ToolExecutionResult(success: false, output: 'x', policyDenied: true)` would close this gap.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `lib/.../feedback_extraction_service.dart:454‚Äì469` ‚Äî `_classifyTextSentiment` has the neutral-result path (equal positive and negative scores) only weakly covered by the "no keywords ‚Üí neutral" case. The `positiveScore == negativeScore > 0` balanced path (e.g., one positive and one negative keyword in the same text) is not directly asserted in any test. Lines 1386‚Äì1418 test mixed but always with an unequal count.

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/.../agent_template_service.dart:709‚Äì731` ‚Äî `profileInUse` has all three branches covered (template reference, version reference, agent reference) in the Glados test (line 4329), but the concurrent-fetch error path (when `getVersionHistory` throws for one template) is untested. This branch in `Future.wait` is swallowed silently and a failure would produce a false-negative `false` return. **RESOLVED:** (adapted) the premise was wrong ‚Äî `Future.wait` rethrows, nothing is swallowed; the new test pins the actual contract: a throwing version-history fetch propagates out of `profileInUse`.

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/.../soul_document_service.dart:405‚Äì443` ‚Äî `assignSoulToTemplate` has a "soft-delete existing links, then create new one" path and a "sole link already matches ‚Üí no-op" path. The tests cover the no-op (line 231) and replace (line 213) cases, but there is no test for the "multiple stale links (sync-race residue)" scenario: when `existingLinks.length > 1` and one of them already points at `soulId`. The `if (existingLinks.length == 1 && ...)` guard means this is a distinct, untested code path. **RESOLVED:** done ‚Äî the sync-race test seeds a stale + matching link pair and asserts both are soft-deleted and exactly one fresh link is written (the no-op guard must not fire).

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/.../soul_document_service.dart` ‚Äî no Glados property tests exist anywhere in `soul_document_service_test.dart` (919 lines). The service has several pure helper invariants: `_requireNonBlank` (throws on any blank/whitespace string), `getVersionHistory` sort order (newest-first), `rollbackToVersion` archives-then-reactivates invariant. These are prime Glados candidates that the example-based tests only partially exercise. **RESOLVED:** done (adapted) ‚Äî a Glados property drives the `_requireNonBlank` guard across whitespace variants √ó all three required fields; the version-history sort lives in the repository (the service is a pass-through), and rollback's archive-then-reactivate is already pinned by example tests.

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/.../change_set_notification_service.dart:78‚Äì100` ‚Äî `_resolveSupportedLocale` and `_isExactLocaleMatch` are pure functions. The test file has one `testWidgets` test for an unsupported locale (line 211) and one implicit via the default locale in earlier tests, but the "language code match without script/country" path (second `for` loop in `_resolveSupportedLocale`) is tested only via the `'zz'` language, not for a known-language with differing country/script (e.g., `'en-AU'`). This is an edge-case gap. **RESOLVED:** done (adapted) ‚Äî exposed that `localeTestValue` cannot fake `ui.PlatformDispatcher.instance`, so a `resolveSupportedLocaleForTest` seam now tests the resolver directly: exact match, de-AT ‚Üí de language-only match, and the zz fallback to the first supported locale.

- [x] **[LOW]** `lib/.../agent_template_service.dart:396‚Äì447` ‚Äî `deleteTemplate` soft-deletes the template, head, and all versions. The test (Glados, line 2476) covers empty `versionSlots`, non-version entities, archived vs active versions. However, the scenario where `getTemplate` returns `null` inside the transaction (i.e., the template was concurrently deleted) causes a silent no-op (`if (template == null) return;` at line 413). This path is not directly asserted and relies on the general Glados coverage.
  **RESOLVED:** (stale/verified) a dedicated example test already pins this path: `deleteTemplate` group ‚Üí `'no-op when template does not exist'` stubs `getEntity('missing')` ‚Üí `null` (so the in-transaction `getTemplate` returns `null`), calls `deleteTemplate('missing')`, and asserts `verifyNever(() => mockSync.upsertEntity(any()))`. The `if (template == null) return;` branch is therefore directly and explicitly covered, not merely via Glados. No change needed.

- [x] **[LOW]** `lib/.../project_recommendation_service.dart:149‚Äì176` ‚Äî `_parseSteps` handles `rawSteps is! List` (returns `[]`), `rawStep is! Map` (skip), blank title (skip), and valid metadata. The Glados test (line 383) covers these via generated step slots. However, the `rationale` and `priority` whitespace-trim edge case (a string of only spaces) is only covered for the `title` field; `rationale = '   '` and `priority = '   '` would produce `null` in the drafts but are not directly asserted.
  **RESOLVED:** added a `validWithWhitespaceMetadata` step slot to the recording Glados generator: a valid title with `rationale: ' \t '` and `priority: '   '` (both whitespace-only but non-empty). Its expected draft is title-only, so the existing per-draft assertions `entity.rationale == draft.rationale` (null) and `entity.priority == draft.priority` (null) now pin the `.trim().isNotEmpty` false branch for whitespace strings on both fields ‚Äî including that a blank priority is never routed through `.toUpperCase()`. (The pre-existing `validWithEmptyMetadata` slot already covered whitespace `rationale` and empty-string `priority`; this fills the remaining whitespace-only `priority` gap.)

## Test execution speed opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/agents/service/agent_template_service_test.dart` ‚Äî Seven Glados property tests use `numRuns` of 160‚Äì220 (`glados.ExploreConfig(numRuns: 220)` at line 1624, `numRuns: 180` at lines 2478, 2767, 3396, 4145, 4674, and `numRuns: 160` at lines 3056, 4145). Each Glados iteration rebuilds the full mock graph via `setUp` (line 1485) which creates a `MockAgentRepository` with a dozen `when(...)` stubs. Because `setUp` runs once per Glados iteration, each of the 7 properties pays the full mock-initialization cost on every run. Moving the mock construction into `setUpAll` (registering stubs once and resetting state between iterations via `reset(mock)`) would remove repetitive object allocation from the hot inner loop. **RESOLVED (assessed, no change):** the premise is incorrect ‚Äî `setUp` runs once per `test()` callback, and a Glados property registers a single `test()` whose explore loop runs inside the body; mocks are not rebuilt per iteration.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/agents/service/change_set_confirmation_service_test.dart:2138` ‚Äî Single Glados property at `numRuns: 180` (line 2138) runs inside a `setUp` that wires several mock stubs. Same pattern as above: mock objects rebuilt on every Glados iteration. Extract mock construction to `setUpAll` and use `reset(mock)` between iterations. **RESOLVED (assessed, no change):** the premise is incorrect ‚Äî `setUp` runs once per `test()` callback, and a Glados property registers a single `test()` whose explore loop runs inside the body; mocks are not rebuilt per iteration.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/features/agents/service/suggestion_retraction_service_test.dart:360,1619` ‚Äî Two Glados properties (`numRuns: 220`, `numRuns: 120`). The `setUp` at the top level builds a `MockAgentRepository` and multiple stubs for every iteration. Shared `setUpAll` + `reset(mock)` would reduce per-iteration overhead. **RESOLVED:** (adapted) the premise is wrong ‚Äî `setUp` runs once per Glados *test*, not per iteration; the 220-run budget was trimmed to 180 per the numRuns policy.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/features/agents/service/task_agent_service_test.dart:262` ‚Äî Glados property at `numRuns: 220`. The comment `// syncService stubs set up in setUp` confirms stubs are rebuilt per iteration. Same optimization applies. **RESOLVED:** (adapted) same factual correction; numRuns 220 ‚Üí 180.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/features/agents/service/improver_agent_service_test.dart:308` ‚Äî Glados property at `numRuns: 260` (highest in this directory). Mock setup in `setUp` is repeated 260 times. This is the single most-repeated mock initialization in the service test suite; moving it to `setUpAll` would cut setup calls by ~250. **RESOLVED:** (adapted) same factual correction; numRuns 260 ‚Üí 180 (the policy ceiling).

- [x] **[LOW]** `test/features/agents/service/feedback_extraction_service_test.dart:852` ‚Äî Glados property at `numRuns: 180`. The service under test (`FeedbackExtractionService`) has no I/O mocks ‚Äî it is constructed from pure-data inputs. This test is already relatively fast, but verifying that no async database calls are made per iteration (the service only parses/classifies text) would confirm there is no hidden I/O cost.
  **RESOLVED:** (assessed false, no change) the premise is incorrect. `FeedbackExtractionService` takes an `AgentRepository` + `AgentTemplateService`, and the Glados test at line 850 drives `service.extract(...)`, which on every iteration makes mocked async repository calls ‚Äî `getRecentDecisionsForTemplate`, a `Future.wait` fan-out of `getEntity` for change sets/payloads, `getWakeRunsForTemplate`, plus `templateService.getRecentInstanceObservations/Reports`. The service is not "constructed from pure-data inputs" and the path is not pure text parsing, so a `verifyNever(DB call)` assertion would *fail* ‚Äî there is genuine (mocked, in-memory) I/O per iteration by design. numRuns is already 180 (within the 80‚Äì180 policy band); no change warranted.

---

## Summary

- **Oversized implementation files:** 1 (`agent_template_service.dart` at 973 lines ‚Äî at the hard ceiling; needs splitting along evolution/seeding/metrics seams).
- **Oversized test files:** 4 (`agent_template_service_test.dart` 4827, `change_set_confirmation_service_test.dart` 2526, `feedback_extraction_service_test.dart` 2191, `suggestion_retraction_service_test.dart` 1708 ‚Äî all 3‚Äì5√ó the 500-line preference).
- **Weak/copy-paste tests:** 3 clusters (three near-identical keyword-sentiment tests in `feedback_extraction_service_test.dart`; two sets of out-of-bounds index tests in `change_set_confirmation_service_test.dart`; the `for`-loop body duplication for `RunningTimerUpdateFailure` variants).
- **Glados candidates:** 5 genuine pure-logic gaps (`_classifyTextSentiment`, `_argsContainExplanatoryContext`, `nextSessionNumber` fold, `_describeArgsForLog` arg-name filter, `_locateAll` scan invariant).
- **Biggest opportunity:** Splitting `agent_template_service.dart` (and mirroring its 4827-line test) into evolution/seeding/metrics sub-services is the single highest-leverage change ‚Äî it unblocks comprehensible test files, enables targeted Glados coverage on evolution-data gathering, and brings both impl and tests within the preferred size bounds.
